Exercise description: Rolling ball towards chest until you feel astretch in the low back.

Exercise group: Bilateral hip flexion/low back flexion stretch
Exercise description: While laying on the back pull both knees uptowards the chest. Repeat.

Exercise group: Bilateral hip flexion/low back flexion stretch
Exercise description: While in a seated position, cross your armsover your chest and slowly curl your backforward in order to round your upper back.

Exercise group: Bilateral hip flexion/low back flexion stretch
Exercise description: Sit as tall as possible. Let your trunk fallforward toward the ground. Hold your hands onthe ground once you get to the bottom and leteverything relax.

Exercise group: Bilateral hip flexion/low back flexion stretch
Exercise description: While sitting in a chair, begin by slowly bendingforward and reaching your hands towards thefloor. Bend your trunk and head forward anddown. Hold, then return to starting position andrepeat.
Laying on your back with your knees bent,draw in your belly button as if putting on a tightpair of pants. It should feel like you areflattening or pushing your back into thetable/bed.
Contract your lower abdominals as if you weretrying to lift one leg from the table. Initiate themovement but do no lift foot greater than 1inch from the table. Repeat opposite side.
While lying on your back with your knees bent,slowly slide your heel forward on the floor/bedand then slide it back. Use your stomachmuscles to keep your spine from moving.

Start by lying on your back with knees bentand flattening your pelvis and low back downto the floor. Brace and hold this position as youraise up one leg at a time to TABLE TOPposition as shown. Your hips and knees shouldbe bent to approximately 90 degree angles. Donot allow your low back to arch upwards for theentire exercise.Next, lower one leg down close to touching thefloor. Return that leg to 90-90 position and thenrepeat on the other side.
While lying on your back with your knees bent,slowly raise up one foot a few inches and thenset it back down. Next, perform on your otherleg. Use your stomach muscles to keep yourspine from moving.

While lying on your back with both knees bent,stabilize your spine by bracing your abdominalmuscles. Hold this contraction as you slowlylower one knee to the side. Your pelvis shouldnot move.You can place your thumbs on your pelvicbone to get feedback of any movements thatoccur. If your pelvis moves too much, then nexttime lower the leg less to maintain goodcontrol.

Lay on back with knees bent. Tightenabdominals as if a bowling ball were about tobe dropped on abdomen. Be sure to not holdyour breath. Do not tighten abdominals in away that will change the neutral position of thespine. Slowly and under control, let one leg fallout to the side while maintaining pelvis inneutral. Return leg to starting position andperform on the other side. Relax.Make sure each repetition starts with a newabdominal contraction and alternate thestarting side.

Start with both knees bent, and your feet onthe floor. Tighten your abdominal muscles, likeyou are drawing your mid-section to your bellybutton. Your ribs should pull together and downslightly. Lift 1 leg to 90 degreed of hip flexion(pic 1), then lift the second leg to 90 degreeship flexion (pic 2). As you lift, make sure youare not letting your back arc. Lower the first legback to the starting position, then lower thesecond leg to the starting position. LIFT-LIFT;LOWER-LOWER. Only do as many as you canunfer GOOD control

Lie down on your back with the feet on thefloor and your knees bent at 90 degrees. Thelow back should remain neutral or flat againstthe table through the whole exercise. Brace theabdominals and slowly raise one leg towardsthe ceiling while maintaining the same 90degree bend through the knee. Stop when thelower leg is parallel with the floor. Slowly lowerthe leg back down to the starting position.Repeat the motion with the other leg. Keep asteady breathing pattern through the wholeexercise.

While lying on your back with your knees andhips bent to 90 degrees, use your stomachmuscles and maintain pelvic neutral position.Do not allow your spine to move.Hold pelvic neutral and then slowly straightenout a leg without touching the floor. At thesame time raise an opposite arm over head.Do not allow your spine to arch during thismovement.Return to starting position and then repeat onthe opposite side.

Lie on your back with your knees bent. Useyour abdominal muscles to press your lowerback into the floor / mat. Hold this while you liftwith your feet off the floor a few inches. Useyour stomach muscles to keep your spine frommoving the entire time.

While lying on your back with your knees bent,raise up both feet and straighten one out infront of you. Then return the leg back andstraighten the other. Use your stomachmuscles to keep your spine from moving.

Start by lying on your back with your kneesbent. Then, brace / tighten your abdominalmuscles so that your spine does not movewhile you straighten out one leg over theground as shown. Return your leg to startingposition and then perform on the other side.Use your abdominal muscles to prevent yourspine from moving the entire time.

This is to be performed while being seated.Press your finger tips into your relaxedabdomen lateral of your navel. Next, tightenand brace your abdomen so that the musclespush your finger tips away from the center ofyour body. Hold and then relax and repeat.

This can be performed standing or lying down.Press your finger tips into your relaxedabdomen lateral of your navel. Next, tightenand brace your abdomen so that the musclespush your finger tips away from the center ofyour body. Hold and then relax and repeat.

While seated on an exercise ball, tighten yourabdominal muscles and then raise a leg andopposite arm. Return limbs back down andthen raise the opposite side. Try and maintaina stable pelvis and spine the entire time.
While lying on your back with your arms out-stretched forward, partially curl up your trunkas you lift your shoulder blades off the ground.Return to lying down and repeat.

While lying on your back with knees bent,tighten your lower abdominal muscles,squeeze your buttocks and then raise yourbuttocks off the floor/bed as creating a "Bridge"with your body. Hold and then lower yourselfand repeat

Lie down on your back and bend your knees so that your feet are flat on the table. Press your heels into the ground to lift your hips off ofthe table. You should stabilize through your core. Return to the starting position controlling your speed.

While lying on your back, tighten your lowerabdominal muscles, squeeze your buttocks, liftyour toes and then raise your buttocks off thefloor/bed as creating a "Bridge" with your body.You should be pressing through your heels theentire time.

While lying on your back, tighten your lowerabdominal muscles, squeeze your buttocksand then raise your buttocks off the floor/bedas creating a "Bridge" with your body.While holding this position, lift one leg whilemaintaining a level pelvis. Set it back to thefloor and then lift the opposite leg

While lying on your back, raise your buttocksoff the floor/bed into a bridge position.Next, raise and straighten one knee so thatonly the other leg is supporting your body.Then, return that leg back to the floor and thenalternate and perform on the other leg.Try and maintain your pelvis level the entiretime.

While lying on your back, place an elastic bandaround your knees and pull your knees apart.Hold this and then tighten your lowerabdominal muscles, squeeze your buttocksand raise your buttocks off the floor/bed ascreating a "Bridge" with your body.

While lying on your back, place a pillowbetween your knees and squeeze the pillow.Hold this and then tighten your lowerabdominal muscles, squeeze your buttocksand raise your buttocks off the floor/bed ascreating a "Bridge" with your body.

Lie on your back with knees bent. Place asmall rubber ball between your knees.Squeeze the ball with your knees and hold thepressure on the ball.While holding this pressure on the ball withyour knees, press through your heels as youraise your buttocks off the floor/bed creating abridge with your body. Return to startingposition and repeat.
While lying on your back with your knees bent,extend one knee as shown.Next, raise your buttocks off the floor/bed.Try and maintain your pelvis level the entiretime.
While lying on the floor, place an exercise ballunder your lower legs and then raise up yourbuttocks.
While lying on your back with knees bent,squeeze a ball between your knees and raiseyour buttocks off the floor/bed.
Start on all 4's and spread legs to make roomfor the belly.Heels can be close together.Sit back onto heels with arms straight outinfront of you.
While in a crawl position, slowly lower yourbuttocks towards your feet until a stretch is feltalong your back and or buttocks.
While on your hand and knees in a crawlposition, slowly lower your buttocks towardsyour feet. Also, lower your chest towards thefloor as you reach out towards the side.

While lying or sitting, raise up your leg with astraight knee. Keep the opposite knee bentwith the foot planted to the ground.
While lying on your back, raise up a knee intoand press it into your hands.
While seated on an exercise ball, slowly raisea foot up off the floor. Return and then lift theopposite side.Maintain a stable and erect spine.Try to move your legs only
Lie down on your back with your knees straightand your feet on the ball. Slowly curl the balltowards your body and return to the startingposition.
While in a crawling position, slowly raise up anarm out in front of you.

Exercise name: QUADRUPED ALTERNATE ARM AND LEGWITH KNEE ELBOW TOUCHES "BIRD DOGELBOW TOUCHES"
Exercise description: While in a crawling position, slowly lift your legand opposite arm upwards.When returning your arm and leg down, do nottouch the floor but instead touch your elbow toyour opposite knee and lift and straighten themagain. Then set them down on the floor. Next,perform on the other side and repeat.

While lying face down and keeping your lowerabdominal muscles tight, slowly raise up anarm and opposite leg. Slowly lower and thenraise the opposite side.Do not allow your spine to move the entiretime.
While lying face down and keeping your lowerabdominal muscles tight, slowly raise up a leg.Slowly lower and then raise the opposite side.Do not allow your spine to move the entiretime.

Lying on your back with your knees bent,gently rotate your spine as you move yourknees to the side and then reverse directionsand move your knees to the other side. Repeatas you move through a comfortable range ofmotion.

Lying on your back with your knees bent,slowly drop your legs to one side and hold thestretch. Come back to the middle and switchsides. You should feel the stretch in your backon the opposite side that your legs are leaning.Complete this exercise for 3 minutes.
While lying on your back, cross one leg on topof your knee as shown. Next, slowly lower yourknees down towards the ground on the sidewhich has the leg on top.
While lying on your back with an exercise ballunder your lower legs, slowly roll the ball sideto side to gently rotate your back.

Exercise name: SIDE LYING QUADRATUS LUMBORUMSTRETCH - QL STRETCH
Exercise description: Start by lying on your side with your kneesbent. Next, lower your legs off the front of thetable/bed and allow your pelvis to bend for astretch to your lower back.
While in a seated position, raise your arm overhead and bend your body to the opposite sidefor a stretch. Hold, return to starting positionand repeat.

Exercise name: SQUAT WITH HIP HINGE--HIP AND BACK DISSASOCIATION DRILL
Exercise description: When squatting, bend over at the waist, tightenyour stomach muscles by drawing in yournavel and keep your back straight whilebending at your hips. This will protect yourback from excessive loads.Your buttock should lower behind your feet asif you are going to sit on a seat. Emphasizeyour weight going through your heels.Also, for good knee alignment, do not let yourknees pass in front of your toes and keep yourknee in line with your 2nd toe (next to the bigtoe) as it bends.

Exercise name: HIP AND BACK DISASSOCIATION DRILL--LEAN FORWARD WITH HIP HINGE
Exercise description: When leaning forward, bend over at the waist,tighten your stomach muscles by drawing inyour navel and keep your back straight whilebending at your hips. This will protect yourback from excessive loads.Your buttock should lower behind your feet asif you are going to sit on a seat. Emphasizeyour weight going through your heels.Also, for good knee alignment, do not let yourknees pass in front of your toes and keep yourknee in line with your 2nd toe (next to the bigtoe) as it bends.
Reach down and pick up a Kettlebell. Be surethat your back is flat the entire time. Hinge atyour hips, do not bend at the spine.
Lying face down, slowly press up and propyourself up on your elbows. Hold, lower backdown and repeat.
Lying face down, slowly press up and archyour back using your arms in straight position.

Lie down on your stomach with your hands in apushup ready position. Push up onto yourhands and straighten your elbows; do not letyour hips raise off of the table. This motion willextend your back. Return to the startingposition.

Start with your hands in push up position.Press your chest up as high as you can usingonly your arms. Try to keep your hips on themat and relax your stomach.Stop the exercise and return to prone on elbowposition if the pain or numbness moves furtheraway from the lower back.

Prone on Elbows - Lay propped up on yourelbows for 2-3 minutes. The pain or numbnessshould move closer to the lower back. Press your chest upas high as you can using only your arms. Place elbows on wall. Allow hips to fall towardswall.
Standing lumbar extensionHands on the hips, keeping your gaze forward,gently press your hips forward to tolerance andreturn to neutral spinal position.*Perform with wall behind you for support

Exercise description: With band secured to door or other stableobject, begin by facing 90 deg away fromband. While holding band, take 2-3 steps awayfrom anchor point (without twisting) untiltension is felt. Extend or "press" arms forwardas shown while gently bending the knees aspictured above. Hold before returning tostarting position. Repeat facing oppositedirection.

Lie on back with knees bent, feet flat and legs shoulder width apart. Imagine a glass of wateron your belly button. Tilt your pelvis to spill the water onto your chest. Hold up to 10 seconds. Relax. Tilt pelvis to spill water in between yourlegs. Hold up to 10 seconds. Relax. Repeat

Lie on your back on a firm surface with knee scomfortably bent (top picture). Then flatten back against the table while contracting abdominal muscles as if pulling belly button toward ribs (bottom picture).

Exercise description: Lie on your back and perform a pelvic tilt. Thiscan be done by using your stomach muscles topress your low back into the floor/mat.Hold this while squeezing a small rubberball/rolled up towel or folded pillow betweenyour knees.Relax both your knees and your stomachmuscles and then repeat.

In supine with knees bent and feet flat on flooryou want to activate your TRANSVERSE ABmuscle- visualize tilting your pelvis back justslightly and drawing in your belly button towardyour back bone. (basically like trying to zip upa tight pair of pants)You should feel the transverse Ab muscle pullin and away from your fingers. HOLD andrepeat

During the movement your pelvisrolls back (posterior tilt) and then back to arelaxed position.Repeat this with smooth controlled motions.
